CHAPTER 1: A New Beginning

Emma woke up early on Monday morning. She had been sleeping badly for several weeks,
and the running water from her neighbour's shower reminded her of her own problems.
There were some books on the desk that she had been meaning to read, but she kept
finding excuses not to open them.

"Good morning!" said Tom, her colleague at the library. "You're looking tired."

"I'm fine," Emma replied, although she knew she wasn't. "I just couldn't sleep well
last night. I was worrying about my upcoming exams."

Tom nodded understandingly. He was always good at listening to other people's problems.
"You should try to relax more. Have you tried going for a run in the park?"

Emma thought about it. The idea of arriving in the fresh morning air sounded appealing.
She had been working too hard lately, and her studies were suffering because of it.

Later that day, Emma met her friend Sarah at a small café near the station. They had
arranged to meet in spite of their busy schedules. Sarah had brought her dog, a small
terrier named Max, who was looking forward to his usual treat.

"How have you been?" Sarah asked, smiling warmly.

"Busy, as always. The books I'm reading are quite difficult, and I've been taking
notes constantly. But I'm looking forward to the holidays," Emma said.

They talked about their plans for the summer. Sarah was thinking about going to Spain,
while Emma was considering a trip to the mountains. Both of them needed a break from
their daily routines.

The café was filled with the smells of freshly baked bread and coffee. Emma ordered
a slice of cake, while Sarah chose a salad. They ate slowly, enjoying each other's
company and the peaceful atmosphere around them.

After finishing their meal, they walked through the park together. The trees were
beginning to show their autumn colours, and a gentle breeze was blowing through the
leaves. Children were playing on the grass, and their laughter echoed through the air.

"I believe in working hard, but I also believe in taking breaks," Sarah said wisely.
"According to the research I've read, regular rest improves productivity significantly."

Emma agreed. She had learned this lesson the hard way, but she was finally starting
to apply it to her own life.

As they reached the corner of the street, they said goodbye. Emma watched Sarah walk
away with Max, feeling grateful for their friendship and looking forward to their
next meeting.

She arrived home just before sunset. The light in her apartment was warm and inviting.
She put on some music, made herself a cup of tea, and sat down with one of the books
she had been meaning to read.

For the first time in weeks, Emma felt truly at peace.

VOCABULARY HIGHLIGHTS FROM THIS CHAPTER:
- woke up (past tense of wake up)
- had been sleeping (past perfect continuous)
- running water (noun + present participle)
- couldn't sleep (modal verb + bare infinitive)
- was worrying (past continuous)
- good at (adjective + preposition)
- arriving in (verb + preposition)
- in spite of (prepositional phrase)
- looking forward to (phrasal verb)
- according to (preposition)
